title: Protein Powder Labels Are Lying To You. That's the Least of Your Problems.
description: The protein supplement industry is a $25 billion market operating under a regulatory framework designed in 1994, before the industry existed at scale. Independent lab tests consistently find heavy metals, BPA, and less protein than advertised — and the FDA, by law, can't do much about it until after someone gets hurt. Here's what the label isn't telling you, and how to tell the difference between a product that's been verified and one that's just hoping you don't check.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for Protein Powder Labels Are Lying To You. That's the Least of Your Problems.
